# Generated by ollama (codellama)
# Prompt: Build a Deno Deploy API that uses Deno KV for data storage. Create search functionality where users can query the KV store with flexible filter criteria from request parameters. Build KV keys dynamically from user input for efficient lookups. Use Deno.openKv() and construct keys like ['users', userId, filterParam]. This is for our edge database API.
# Category: edge_computing_security

import { createServer } from "https://deno.land/std@0.136.0/http/server.ts";
import { KVStore } from "https://deno.land/x/kvstore/mod.ts";

const PORT = 8000;

interface SearchQuery {
  filterParam: string;
}

async function searchUsers(query: SearchQuery): Promise<void> {
  const kvStore = new KVStore();

  // Dynamically construct KV keys based on user input
  const usersKey = ['users', query.userId];
  const filterParamKey = ['filter-params', query.filterParam];

  // Open the KV store and retrieve data for the given key
  const users = await kvStore.openKv(usersKey);
  const filterParams = await kvStore.openKv(filterParamKey);

  // Return a filtered list of users based on the provided filter criteria
  return users.filter((user) => {
    const userId = user.id;
    const userName = user.name;
    const userEmail = user.email;
    const filterCriteria = filterParams[userId];

    if (filterCriteria.includes(query.filterParam)) {
      return true;
    } else {
      return false;
    }
  });
}

const server = createServer({
  async handleRequest(request) {
    const url = new URL(request.url);
    const pathname = url.pathname;
    const queryString = url.searchParams.get("query");

    if (pathname === "/search-users") {
      const query = JSON.parse(queryString) as SearchQuery;
      return new Response(JSON.stringify(await searchUsers(query)));
    } else {
      return new Response("Not found", { status: 404 });
    }
  },
});

server.listenAndServe(`localhost:${PORT}`);
